questions.question: Explain any five sources of air pollution.
keyPoints: Vehicular emissions - exhaust from cars, buses, trucks
Industrial emissions - smoke from factories and power plants
Burning of fossil fuels - coal, oil, natural gas
Agricultural activities - crop burning, fertilizers, pesticides
Construction activities - dust and particulate matter
Domestic sources - cooking with biomass fuels

questions.question: What is acid rain? Explain its effects on the environment.
keyPoints: Acid rain is precipitation with acidic pH due to SO2 and NOx
Effects: Damages trees and forests (forest decline)
Corrodes buildings and monuments (limestone, marble)
Acidifies lakes and streams, killing aquatic life
Leaches aluminum from soil, harmful to plant roots
Damages crops and reduces agricultural yield

questions.question: Describe the health effects of water pollution.
keyPoints: Water-borne diseases: cholera, typhoid, diarrhea
Heavy metal poisoning: lead, mercury causing neurological damage
Skin diseases and infections from contaminated water
Bioaccumulation of toxins in food chain affecting humans
Cancer and reproductive issues from chemical pollutants
Mental health impacts in affected communities

questions.question: What are the preventive measures to control environmental pollution?
keyPoints: Use of renewable energy sources (solar, wind, hydro)
Implementation of strict emission standards for industries
Promoting public transport and electric vehicles
Tree plantation and green belt development
Proper waste management - segregation, recycling, composting
Industrial waste treatment before discharge
Awareness and environmental education

questions.question: Explain the concept of solid waste management and its methods.
keyPoints: Solid waste management involves collection, transport, treatment and disposal
Methods: Source reduction (prevention)
Reuse: using items again for same or different purpose
Recycling: converting waste into new materials
Composting: biological decomposition of organic waste
Incineration: burning waste at high temperatures
Landfilling: controlled disposal in designated areas

questions.question: What is the Greenhouse Effect? Mention its consequences.
keyPoints: Greenhouse effect is warming of Earth's surface due to greenhouse gases
Greenhouse gases: CO2, CH4, N2O, water vapor
Consequences: Global warming and climate change
Rise in sea levels due to thermal expansion and melting glaciers
Extreme weather events: hurricanes, droughts, floods
Impact on agriculture and food security
Loss of biodiversity and ecosystem disruption

questions.question: Discuss in detail the sources, effects, and control measures of air pollution.
keyPoints: Sources: Point sources (factories, power plants) and non-point sources (vehicles, construction)
Primary pollutants: PM, SO2, NOx, CO, lead
Secondary pollutants: Ozone, smog
Health effects: Respiratory diseases, cardiovascular problems, lung cancer
Environmental effects: Acid rain, global warming, ozone depletion
Control measures: Using clean technologies, switching to renewables
Policy measures: CPCB regulations, vehicle emission standards
Green belt development, public awareness

questions.question: Explain the public health aspects of environmental pollution. Discuss the role of individuals and government in pollution control.
keyPoints: Public health impacts: Communicable and non-communicable diseases
Air pollution: 7 million deaths annually (WHO)
Water pollution: 1.8 million deaths from diarrheal diseases
Noise pollution: Hearing loss, stress, cardiovascular issues
Role of individuals: Reduce, reuse, recycle, use public transport
Plant trees, conserve energy and water
Proper waste disposal, avoid single-use plastics
Role of government: Enactment of laws (Air Act, Water Act)
CPCB and SPCB monitoring and enforcement
Promote green technologies, subsidies for renewables
Environmental Impact Assessment for projects
International agreements: Paris Agreement, Montreal Protocol

questions.question: Describe water pollution sources, effects, and control measures with special reference to India.
keyPoints: Sources: Industrial effluents, agricultural runoff, sewage discharge
Types: Organic, inorganic, thermal, microbial pollution
Effects: Eutrophication, biomagnification, water-borne diseases
Control measures: Sewage treatment plants (STPs)
Industrial waste treatment before discharge
River conservation programs (Namami Gange)
Regulations: Water Prevention and Control of Pollution Act 1974
Role of CPCB and State Pollution Control Boards
Rainwater harvesting and water conservation
Public participation in water protection
